patch.value: Surface settings_read_invalid_schema warning proactively on affected commands

On invalid settings.json, readSettings() silently falls back to defaults; create/list/search use readSettings (not WithMetadata) so the warning is generated but never shown; a typo breaks behavior with no explanation.
main.ts dispatch uses readSettingsWithMetadata and emits settings_read_* warnings to stderr (or a pm health check catches it); corrupt-settings test confirms the warning reaches output.

On invalid settings.json, readSettings() silently falls back to defaults; create/list/search use readSettings (not WithMetadata) so the warning is generated but never shown; a typo breaks behavior with no explanation.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Cluster: agent self-repair via shared remediation registry (health --json remediation_map + validate --fix-hints + settings warning surfacing)

patch.value: main.ts threads settings_read_* warnings through the discovery snapshot (zero extra reads) and prints them once per command to stderr; readSettings fallback to defaults is no longer silent.
create/list/search surface settings_read_invalid_schema/json instead of silently using defaults.
pm list with corrupt settings.json prints the stderr warning; stdout/--json stays clean; verified in sandbox.
Settings-read warnings now surfaced to stderr
